title: cut list ignores the saw kerf
description: ran a 96in board into 5 pieces and the numbers dont add up. i think its not subtracting the blade width per cut. mine eats like 1/8 each pass.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: let me type feet and inches not just inches
description: typing 47.5 for a board is fine but half the time i measure in feet. would be nice to punch in 3ft 11.5in. small ask.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: print view cuts off the last row
description: tried to print the cut list to take to the shop and the bottom board got cut off the page. printed from my phone if that matters.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: board length dropdown only goes to 96in
description: hardwood store sells 10 and 12 foot boards too. cant enter anything over 96. would help to type any length.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: rounds fractions weird, 3/16 shows as 0.19
description: i think in fractions not decimals. showing 0.19 instead of 3/16 makes me do math at the saw. can it show fractions.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: app forgets my last cut list when i close the tab
description: spent a while punching in a big list, closed it by accident, all gone. could it save to the browser so i dont lose it.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: diagram overlaps the numbers on a long board
description: when a board has a lot of cuts the little diagram runs into the measurements underneath. hard to read which piece is which.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: let me name a cut list so i can tell them apart
description: i do a kitchen job and a deck job in the same week, would be nice to label each list so i know which is which.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: optimizer wastes a whole board sometimes
description: gave it a list and it left almost a full board empty when it couldve packed tighter. might be worth a second pass.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: add a note field per board for grain or defects
description: id like to jot down which board has a knot to work around or which face is the show face. just a little text box per row.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: total board feet number is off
description: the board feet total at the bottom doesnt match when i figure it by hand. think its counting the kerf offcuts as usable.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: let me set a default kerf so i dont retype 1/8 every time
description: since you added kerf, could it remember my blade width. mine is always 1/8, tired of typing it each list.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: metric toggle rounds mm too hard
description: flipped to mm to help a buddy and it rounded everything to whole mm. lost the half mm which matters on tight joinery. trying the new branch for you.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: cant add a second board size in the same list
description: i buy some 6ft and some 8ft stock, but it only lets me set one stock length for the whole run. would love mixed stock.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: buttons too small to tap with work gloves on
description: half the time on site i got gloves on and the add-cut button is tiny. bigger tap targets would help a lot.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: readme doesnt say how to run it locally
description: nate wanted to poke at it on my laptop and the readme skips the setup steps. reading through it, theres no run instructions.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: export the cut list to a pdf i can email myself
description: print works but id rather save a pdf and email it to the guys or keep it. any chance of an export button.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: dark background hard to read outside in the sun
description: on the job site in daylight the dark theme is impossible to read on my phone. a light high contrast mode would help.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: wrong piece count when two cuts are the same length
description: asked for two 24in pieces and it only listed one in the diagram even though the total was right. minor display bug.
author: jmcdaniel-wood

title: add a waste percentage so i know how much im throwing out
description: curious how much of each board ends up as offcut. a little waste percent at the bottom would be a nice touch.
author: jmcdaniel-wood
